how_to_reproduce: Eval__Dataset=juristcu Eval__JurisTcuMaxQueries=50 Rag__TopK=10 ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Testing dotnet run --project src/LexRag.Api ; then POST /eval/ablation

configurations.name: dense-only
configurations.description: vector search (cosine, hash embedder), no sparse leg

configurations.name: sparse-only
configurations.description: BM25 keyword leg only, no dense leg

configurations.name: hybrid-no-rerank
configurations.description: RRF fusion of dense + sparse, no reranker

interpretation: Keyless, the dense leg is the FNV-1a hash proxy (not semantic), so dense-only is the weakest config (recall 10%) and fusing it dilutes the sparse leg: hybrid recall (18%) sits below sparse-only (25%). The reranker recovers part of that (18% -> 23%) and gives the best hit-rate (88%), but does not overtake sparse-only on recall in this keyless setup. This is the expected shape when the dense leg is a stand-in: the hybrid + RRF payoff appears with a real semantic embedder, which is exactly where the measured recall lift (26% -> 42%, see eval-datasets.md) comes from. This run is honest evidence of each leg's keyless contribution, not a claim that the hybrid wins without a real embedder.
